reasoning.typography: Inter Tight + JetBrains Mono is functional but utterly generic for dev tools. The serif headline is standard issue SaaS playbook, no typographic identity.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline dominates adequately, but the product UI screenshot bleeds attention without clear focal point. CTAs compete rather than guide.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Swap 'Railway' for Vercel/Render/Fly.io and this works identically. Dark starfield bg, code aesthetic, logo parade — pure category stencil.
reasoning.color: Restrained dark palette with purple accent is disciplined. Accent used sparingly in CTAs. Atmospheric gradient bg adds depth without chaos.
reasoning.density: Generic uniform padding everywhere. The hero is spacious to the point of emptiness, product screenshot floats without content rhythm or confident density.
reasoning.voice: IA is invisible here — just hero CTA + product screenshot + logo parade. No story structure, no editorial POV, pure conversion funnel template.

winning_patterns: Purple accent used with genuine restraint — appears in CTAs and small UI elements only
Atmospheric background imagery (mountains/stars) creates mood without being decorative noise
Clean header navigation with logical grouping
anti_patterns: Completely interchangeable with every dev platform — zero brand personality
Logo parade of customers is the oldest B2B SaaS cliché
Product screenshot lacks focal point or explanatory context
Headline 'Ship software peacefully' is vague positioning poetry
Standard hero-CTA-product-logos template shows no editorial thinking
